title: Bridge Paper: 20 years of the default mode network: A review and synthesis.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Non-fabrication** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Spontaneous thought — mind-wandering, default mode network, creative cognition, involuntary thought.
**Phase context:** spontaneous thought, mind-wandering, creative cognition, default mode network, involuntary thought, daydreaming

Paper: 20 years of the default mode network: A review and synthesis.
DOI: 10.1016/j.neuron.2023.04.023
Abstract: The discovery of the default mode network (DMN) has revolutionized our understanding of the workings of the human brain. Here, I review developments that led to the discovery of the DMN, offer a personal reflection, and consider how our ideas of DMN function have evolved over the past two decades. I summarize literature examining the role of the DMN in self-reference, social cognition, episodic and autobiographical memory, language and semantic memory, and mind wandering.
